extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC won stage 6 by launching his sprint before 1km on the uphill finish, using yellow jersey position and Jumbo-Visma control to neutralize sprint trains.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled 60k+ with 4min breakaway gap, preventing sprint teams organizing for 2k uphill finish sequence.
ROGLIC launched sprint early on uphill finale using GC position to discourage countermoves from pure sprinters going backwards.
PEREZ locked polka dot jersey by taking 4/5 climbs in breakaway, demonstrating breakaway specialist can control mountains before queen stage.

rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: abandoned from 3rd GC after crash, revealing vulnerability in protecting white jersey on transitional stage
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexander: negative: mechanical issues cost time despite regaining peloton, showing equipment fragility at critical moment
rider_signals.COCKER Bryan: positive: 7th place on uphill sprint finish reveals unexpected climbing capacity for pure sprinter type
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: 2nd on uphill finish demonstrates hybrid sprinter strength when pure speed specialists eliminated by gradient
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: 3rd place confirms uphill sprint capability but couldn't match GC rider's power on final 2k climb
